A developer still needs to figure out how to remove the field, which lines constitute the 'hack', and what type annotation to use.

Any correct fix that removes the field, removes the hack, and adds a type annotation would pass regardless of implementation details.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests check for the absence of tie_word_embeddings in class definitions and presence of type annotations — the base commit has these fields present and annotations missing, so a stub/no-op would fail. The agent must actually modify the source files.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is in /repo as solve.sh could be present, and the gold patch is in solution/solve.sh which may exist in the container. The agent could read /solution/solve.sh or the Dockerfile references the exact commit SHA. More importantly, the tests check for specific string patterns (absence of 'tie_word_embeddings' in certain files) that an agent could discover by reading test_outputs.py if accessible.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All three expected behaviors from instruction.md are tested: (1) text sub-configs not defining tie_word_embeddings (test_qwen2vl_text_no_own_tie_field, test_qwen2_5_vl_text_no_own_tie_field), (2) no hack in composite configs (test_downstream_no_tie_propagation_hack), (3) type annotation on ModernVBertForMaskedLM.__init__ (test_modernvbert_init_type_hint, test_modular_init_type_hint).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (Gates pass_to_pass repo_tests, Gates pass_to_pass static, Fail-to-pass pr_diff), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining what each test checks and why the approach was chosen.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves removing fields and adding type annotations in Python config/model files, not producing structured data output.

no_typos.reason: All file paths, class names, method names, and field names in instruction.md match those in the actual codebase and test files without typos.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md explicitly names the exact files, exact classes, and exact fields to modify (e.g., 'Qwen2VLTextConfig defines tie_word_embeddings as a class-level dataclass field', 'ColQwen2Config.__post_init__ workaround hack', 'ModernVBertForMaskedLM.__init__ is missing a type annotation on its config parameter'). config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task involving Python source file modifications, not configuration file edits.
